lessons.subject: Life Skills
lessons.title: Grade 6 Beginning knowledge and personal well-being — Core teaching
learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to beginning knowledge and personal well-being.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body: In this lesson learners study beginning knowledge and personal well-being using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to beginning knowledge and personal well-being. Key vocabulary: Beginning, knowledge, personal, well-being. Discuss respectful choices, health habits and community responsibility using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 6.LS.1.1 covers Beginning knowledge and personal well-being. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.
lessons.explanation: In this lesson learners study beginning knowledge and personal well-being using clear classroom examples. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to beginning knowledge and personal well-being. Key vocabulary: Beginning, knowledge, personal, well-being. Discuss respectful choices, health habits and community responsibility using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 6.LS.1.1 covers Beginning knowledge and personal well-being. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 6 learner show respect while learning about beginning knowledge and personal well-being?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on beginning knowledge and personal well-being?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice is wise in class?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps you think back?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.C: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which task helps you learn more?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Ignore feedback from the teacher
options.D: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for beginning knowledge and personal well-being and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 6 example about beginning knowledge and personal well-being and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 6.LS.1.1 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect beginning knowledge and personal well-being to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for beginning knowledge and personal well-being. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life skills.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about beginning knowledge and personal well-being without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life skills learning at home.

source_citations.citation_text: CAPS reference 6.LS.1.1 covers Beginning knowledge and personal well-being.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 6 learner show respect while learning about creative arts?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on creative arts?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice is wise in class?

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.B: Copy another learner without understanding
options.C: Leave the activity before it is completed
options.D: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps you think back?

options.A: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.B: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which task helps you learn more?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When topic vocabulary confusion appears, return to the worked example for creative arts and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 6 example about creative arts and teach it to a partner using the standard approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 6.LS.2.2 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect creative arts to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for creative arts. Use the standard variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life skills.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about creative arts without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life skills learning at home.

Key vocabulary: Physical, education, SKILLS, GRADES. Discuss respectful choices, health habits and community responsibility using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: --- page 6 ---

CAPS
CONTENTS
SECTION 1:NATIONAL CURRICULUM AND ASSESSMENT POLICY STATEMENT FOR LIFE SKILL 
INTERMEDIATE PHASE GRADES 4 – 6 ........................................................ Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 6 learner show respect while learning about physical education?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on physical education?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice is wise in class?

options.A: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.B: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps you think back?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which task helps you learn more?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for physical education and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 6 example about physical education and teach it to a partner using the step by step approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 6.LS.3.3 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect physical education to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for physical education. Use the step by step variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life skills.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about physical education without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life skills learning at home.

Key vocabulary: Physical, education, SKILLS, GRADES. Discuss respectful choices, health habits and community responsibility using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: --- page 6 ---
LIFE SKILLS GRADES 4-6
1
CAPS
CONTENTS
SECTION 1:NATIONAL CURRICULUM AND ASSESSMENT POLICY STATEMENT FOR LIFE SKILL 
INTERMEDIATE PHASE GRADES 4 – 6 ........................................................ Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 6 learner show respect while learning about physical education?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on physical education?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

practice_questions.question_text: Which choice is wise in class?

options.A: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
options.B: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
options.C: Copy another learner without understanding
options.D: Leave the activity before it is completed

practice_questions.explanation: Responsible learners follow rules and seek help.

practice_questions.question_text: Which question helps you think back?

options.A: How can I avoid the task completely?
options.B: What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
options.C: Who can I blame if the task is difficult?
options.D: Why should I skip checking my work?

practice_questions.explanation: Reflection links learning to real use.

practice_questions.question_text: Which task helps you learn more?

options.A: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.B: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.C: Create a new example and teach the method to a partner
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for physical education and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 6 example about physical education and teach it to a partner using the step by step approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 6.LS.4.3 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect physical education to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for physical education. Use the step by step variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life skills.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about physical education without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life skills learning at home.

options.B: Repeat the same answer without thinking
options.C: Skip the vocabulary for the topic
options.D: Ignore feedback from the teacher

practice_questions.explanation: Teaching a new example shows deeper understanding.

answer_key.correct_answer_text: Listen, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure
What did I learn and how can I use it at home or school?
Create a new example and teach the method to a partner

remediation_hints.hint_text: When procedure without conceptual understanding appears, return to the worked example for health, safety and environmental responsibility and solve one smaller part before attempting the full question.
remediation_hints.example: Use the checklist in the lesson and explain each step to a partner.

extension_prompts: Create a new Grade 6 example about health, safety and environmental responsibility and teach it to a partner using the visual approach.
Write two exam-style questions for 6.LS.4.4 and explain why your marking memo is correct.
Connect health, safety and environmental responsibility to a real situation in your community and justify your answer with evidence.
lessons.teacher_notes: Pacing: 15 minutes teaching, 10 minutes guided practice, 10 minutes independent practice for health, safety and environmental responsibility. Use the visual variant materials first, then move to the practice set. Common errors to watch: topic vocabulary confusion, procedure without conceptual understanding. Assessment cue: ask learners to explain one method aloud before marking the exit ticket. Subject focus: life skills.
lessons.parent_notes: Ask your child to teach you one example about health, safety and environmental responsibility without looking at the notes. Help them restate the main vocabulary and check whether their answer matches the question. If they are stuck, use a household object to model the idea rather than giving the final answer immediately. This supports life skills learning at home.

source_citations.citation_text: CAPS reference 6.LS.4.4 covers Health, safety and environmental responsibility.

lessons.subject: Life Skills
lessons.title: Grade 6 Health, safety and environmental responsibility — Story context
learning_objectives: Explain core concepts related to health, safety and environmental responsibility.
Use CAPS enquiry, practical, creative or source-work skills for the topic.
Apply the topic in familiar South African classroom and everyday contexts.
lessons.lesson_body: A short South African classroom story introduces health, safety and environmental responsibility before learners practise the skill. CAPS focus: Explain core concepts related to health, safety and environmental responsibility. Key vocabulary: Health, safety, environmental, responsibility. Discuss respectful choices, health habits and community responsibility using the example scenario. Grounding snippet: CAPS reference 6.LS.4.4 covers Health, safety and environmental responsibility. Before finishing, learners restate the method, complete the practice set and compare answers with a partner.

worked_examples.question: Example 1: How can a Grade 6 learner show respect while learning about health, safety and environmental responsibility?
step_by_step_solution: Name the situation in the example.
Identify a respectful action.
Explain why the action helps the group.
Write the answer in one clear sentence.
worked_examples.answer: Listen to others, take turns and use kind words during the activity.

worked_examples.question: Example 2: Which healthy choice supports the lesson on health, safety and environmental responsibility?
step_by_step_solution: Read each option carefully.
Eliminate choices that ignore safety or wellbeing.
Select the option that supports healthy habits.
Explain the choice to a partner.
worked_examples.answer: Wash hands, follow safety rules and ask for help when unsure.

options.A: Ignore instructions and work alone without checking
